observations.reason:
Not implemented for humanitarian reasons. Demand letters were sent instead.

The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of gathering appropriate documentation for the filing of the request for write-off.

No action from management.

observations.managementAction:
An inventory committee was created but failed to conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE.

Report of PPE inventory of each office was already prepared.
observations.reason: Due to absence of prior years’ records and difficulty in implementing all the required procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE, the recommendations were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections at the end of each day or not later than the next banking day to prevent any liability arising from possible losses resulting from unlawful use or application thereof, and for all losses attributable to negligence in keeping of the funds; and 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to completely accomplish the RCDs by filling-out Part A.2 - Collections and Part D - Summary of Collections and Deposits.
observations.reason:
Strict observance of daily deposit was not observed due to intervening activities of the LGU. 

Not prioritized by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive streamline and document its donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: No concrete action was undertaken by the management.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resources Office to re-post/post and fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Sections 443, 474, 476 and 477 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules, and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.
observations.managementAction: The vacant mandatory positions were already published in the CSC Bulletin of Vacant Positions in the Government in the CSC website and were posted in three (3) conspicuous places in the municipality, however the said positions were still unfilled as of December 31, 2024.
observations.reason: No applications were received by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to request immediately from Landbank the bank statements which are not available in weAccess to facilitate the timely and complete preparation of monthly BRS.  Further, direct the concerned accounting personnel to prioritize the preparation of BRS to ensure accuracy of balances reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of tax declarations for land properties to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements in accordance with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report on Physical Count of Local Road Networks for local roads; and
Expedite the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Consider reconstituting the manpower at the GSO office to avoid holding concurrent positions to fully focus their time and effort in the supply and property management of the LGU;
Require the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain Property Cards; and
Henceforth, direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to reconcile Property Cards  and PPE Ledger Cards and regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Consider the cancellation of the contract with the contractor if the consent of the riparian owners or owners of the property adjoining foreshore lands is not secured and return the undisbursed funds to the Provincial Government;
Require the contractor to rectify the damaged diving board, otherwise impose sanctions as provided for under R.A. 9184; and
Direct the contractor to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished and have it checked by the Municipal Engineer to determine the value of work executed.  If the contract is cancelled, recover from the contractor the excess payment, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to ensure that changes in employee salary, new hires, promotions, and retirements are promptly reported to GSIS every month, as required under Section 17 of the IRR of RA No. 8291;
Require the concerned Accounting Staff and Human Resource Management Officer to regularly compare its records with GSIS billing statements to promptly identify and address any discrepancies; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to observe proper cut-off in recording transactions to ensure that these are recorded in the correct period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  ensure that all projects charged under the 20% Development Fund are aligned with the approved AIP and contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Local Government Unit (LGU) in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer to:
Establish procedures for the regular updating of contract wage rates and the accurate processing of salary payments to prevent future inconsistencies; and
Formulate policy guidelines that will set the rates to be paid to job order employees in accordance with existing rules and regulations in hiring of JO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MDRRMO, Budget Officer and LDRRM Council to ensure that charges to the LDRRM Fund are strictly in compliance with Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
Instruct the LDRRM Council to incorporate the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive :
Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Subsequently, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that with a Sanggunian Bayan’s Resolution, the Local Chief Executive promptly close any bank account whose purpose or associated projects have already been served and completed, especially those with balances falling below the required monthly average daily balance.  It is further advised that new account be opened should funds be downloaded to the Municipality for the implementation of new projects.

Alternatively, should the Local Chief Executive choose to keep these accounts active, the management shall ensure that the minimum bank balance required by the bank be maintained to avoid incurrence of bank service charges, otherwise it shall be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to develop measures to ensure that all bank statements are completely retrieved from weAccess at the end of each month; and

Require her to adjust the book reconciling items reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and henceforth record adjusting entries on a timely basis to ensure the accuracy and propriety of the recorded Cash in Bank balance.
observations.managementAction:
An accounting personnel was assigned to download monthly bank statements from we-Access.

The necessary adjusting journal entries were already made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive;

a. Enjoin the Municipal Assessor-Designate to: 

Fast Track the preparation      of the updated Assessment Roll and provide a copy of the Municipal Treasurer as basis for updating the RPTARRs; and

Follow-up approval from the Provincial Assessor of pertinent documents for newly declared/assessed properties for the timely update of the Assessment Roll.

Thereafter direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Update the RPTARs and prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the set-up of the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and 

Prepare a complete list of Delinquent Taxpayers and post in appropriate public places as required under Section 254 of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Assessment Roll was already updated.
observations.reason: Set-up of RPT is still based on Quarterly Report on Real Property Assessments, not on duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year.  Due to significant volume of RPTARs, it was not fully updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledgers for Due to/From Other Funds and reconcile these reciprocal accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained and Journal Entry Vouchers were prepared after reconciliation of reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the the Chief Local Executive:

Comply with the requirement of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 by establishing procedures which will ensure that cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of LDRRMF is actually transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end; and 

Draw correcting entry for the erroneous recording of unexpended balance of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund was made at year-end.

correcting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period adjustments/errors by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable; and

Provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
No prior period errors in CY 2024.

Adequate disclosures were made in the Notes to FS.

Adjusting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant.

The recommendation is only partially implemented. A total of P213,566.45was returned to source agencies. However, balances with no identified source agencies are not yet remitted to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason:
Not prioritized by the LGU.

Not prioritized by the Municipal Accountant due to complexity of procedures and documents required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Include in the AIP for LDRRMF only those PPAs which are responsive to the purpose of the fund; and

Direct the MDRRMO, Budget Officer and GSO-Designate to ensure that charges to LDRRMF is strictly in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Point System to submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report was submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to: 

i. Comply with the posting of the Notice of Delinquency as required under Section 254 of R.A. 7160; and

ii. Update the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers that would include all delinquent accounts.  If necessary, request for augmentation of personnel to regularly prepare and update the list. 

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

i. Prepare an aging of the RPT delinquencies based on the taxpayers Statement of Accounts; and

ii. Recognize allowance for impairment for all long overdue RPT/SET receivables.
observations.managementAction:
The recommendation was complied by the Municipal Treasurer.


The recommendation was complied by the Municipal Treasurer.


The Municipal Accountant cannot prepare an aging of the RPT delinquencies since the Municipal Treasurer has not submitted a Statement of Accounts as basis for the aging.

observations.managementAction: GAD database was created.
Updates were made on the Assessment Roll for only 5 out of 16 barangays

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below P15,000.00 and provide a copy of the JEV to the GSO together with the schedule or list of affected PPE for their subsequent dropping from the property/equipment records.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the above-mentioned provision and effect the necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the asset account and depreciation.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 5% LDRRM fund to strengthen preparations for disaster risk reduction and management in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended the municipal accountant to reclassify the current and non-current portion of the long-term loans payable to Loans Payable – Current, Domestic account and Loans Payable, Long-Term Domestic, respectively and present these payables under the appropriate category in the financial statements.

We recommended that management direct all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ and PO and its supporting documents and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the above-mentioned provision and prepare a reversing entry to correct valuation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the insurance premium covering the whole year be taken up as Prepaid Insurance and only the portion of the prepayment that has been used up be recognized as Insurance Expense. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw an adjusting entry for the reclassification.

We recommended that Management re-post/post and fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Sections 443, 474, 476 and 477 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, for more effective administration of the said offices which in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.

We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concurred municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO concurred with recommend-ation and coordinate with offices for reportorial requirements.
The RHU Inventory In – Charge and the Municipal Accountant concurred to submit inventory report to the Accounting office and record the same upon receipt, respectively.

Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to submit a status report regarding re- assessments results and requested DSWD RX for approval for ineligible beneficiaries subsidized for the cash grant.
observations.reason: A narrative report on its partial assessment was prepared.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office and the RHU Inventory In- charge concurred with the observation and committed to prepare the report and submit the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

Submit complete documentation for cash advances granted;  

Stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials over and above the purposes as stated in COA Circular No. 97-002, section 4.1.4.  Also, purposes of the cash advances granted should be clearly stated, for legal specific purpose and within the provisions set on the aforesaid circular. 

Faithful adherence to the above-cited provision is hereby enjoined, otherwise, disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our recommendations to Management the following:

To prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE; 

To direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain updated Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and

The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.
observations.reason: Current record of PPE are updated except those acquired prior years, thus reiterated

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concurred municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.

We have recommended the management:
Require the RHU In-Charge to submit inventory report upon acquisition of donated drugs and medicines to the Accounting Office;
Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.

We have recommended the management:
Organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC; and
Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon.

We have recommended the management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the pertinent lacking supporting documents in order to establish the propriety of the affected transactions; and
Faithful adherence to the above-cited provision is hereby enjoined, otherwise, disallowed in audit.

We have recommended to management the following: To instruct the property officer to maintain a record of property card for each item of PPE; to conduct physucal count of all PPEs owned by the agency, through its duly designated members of the Inventory Committee, at least once a year and to reconcile accounts between the Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time
observations.managementAction: To conduct actual inventory of current PPE and seek technical assistance from COA so that necessary corrections can be made to LGU records reflecting actual PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommend to Management the following: Faithful adherence to the above-cited provision is hereby enjoined, plan all procurement meticulously and judiciously and justify why Management sorted to Small Value Procurement instead of going through the process off public bidding.

We recommend that management: Observe the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.

We recommended the following: Justify why this expense were granted in cash advance, stop the practices of granting cash advances intended for miscellaneous expenses without legally specific purpose, and faithful adherence to the above-cited provision is hereby enjoined, otherwise, disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: To stop granting cash advances intended for miscellaneous expenses without legally specific purpose and faithful adherence to the above-cited provision

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Justify the delayed completion of various projects and furnish the approved time extensions to our office, direct the Municipal Engineer to prioritize and to fast track the implementation of the LGU's P/P/As, send a written demand to the contractors for the full and satisfactory performance of their obligations under their respective contract, enforce the provisions in the contract particularly the aspect on liquidated damages in congruence with the provisions on liquidated damages under RA 9184 and carefully and judiciously review and re-evaluate the necessary qualifications of the contractor as to its manpower compliment, financial capacity to undertake the contract, availability of the require heavy equipment for the contract and status of prior contract entered into by the supplier/contractor as to how satisfactory they were in their execution or performance of their obligation.
observations.managementAction: To comply with the recommendation.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
Justify unrelated expenses charged to 1% IRA for Children’s Protection;
Charge travelling expenses to General Fund savings if there is any and restore back 1% IRA intended for children’s protection; and
Refrain charging expenses unrelated to the programs, projects and activities allowed to be funded intended for children’s protection, otherwise, subject to disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management that:

1.) Conduct monthly monitoring of liquidation of cash advances.
2.) Refrain from allowing municipal personnel for additional cash advance unless their previous cash advance is settled.
3.) Impose administrative disciplinary action, to municipal officials and employee for the settlement of their outstanding accounts/liquidation of their respective cash advances after proper written demands were made.
observations.reason: Substantial amount of cash advances (current) have been liquidated except for cash advances aged more than a year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management that:

1.) Increase the amount of bond premium using the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates provided in Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
2.) Secure adequate fidelity bond equivalent to the maximum accountability of accountable officer in order to safeguard the assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation and other loss due to fortuitous events.
3.) Monitor properly the change in accountability of each   accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premium conforms to their amount of bond.
4.) Appropriate yearly a budget to cover for the payment of premiums on the renewal of the fidelity bonds both for public funds and public property.

We recommended to Management that:

1.) Disbursing responsibility be allowed only to those who are duly bonded to afford adequate protection to the government. Designated non-bondable disbursing officer shall desist from handling government funds.
 2.) Strictly comply with Section 182 of the GAAM, Volume I, the regulations governing the Fidelity Bonding of government officials and employees under Treasury Order No. 01-95 of the Bureau of Treasury should also be implemented.

We recommended to Management that:

1.) Require the barangay officials to assist municipal Treasury’s Office personnel in delivering Real Property Tax Statements (RPTS) to the 16 barangays of the municipality and to assist in locating the owner of the undelivered RPTS.
2.) Direct the Municipality Treasurer to avail of the civil remedies provided for under R.A. 7160.
3.) Develop other feasible strategies to enhance collections of delinquent taxes from taxpayers.

We recommended to management that:

Management should facilitate the adoption of Payroll-thru-bank System, which is a much safer scheme that precludes hold-ups and similar incidents involving payrolls, and promotes operational efficiency.
observations.managementAction: On process of service provider

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that:

Require the AO to prepare monthly, the Report of Accountability for AFs and submit the same to the municipal treasurer.
2.) Require the municipal treasurer to prepare the consolidated Inventory and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit it to the Unit Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended to management that:

1.) Direct the accounting office to maintain subsidiary ledgers provided for each account to support as per general ledger controlling accounts for easy verification and control of the account, to facilitate review of transactions and events recorded and to obtain reliability of financial records and reports.
2.)  Improve the present accounting system by installing computerized system of the accounting office that will provide adequate data control and present accurate reports.
observations.managementAction: Monthly maintenance of SL except for those cannot be traced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that:

1.   We recommend to management that an Internal Audit Service be created and organized independent of the Accounting Department, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of R.A. No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278. Moreover, it should be required to regularly evaluate and/or monitor the implementation of its internal accounting and operational controls in all aspects of its operations to ensure that reliability of data, assets and records are safeguarded, and that operational efficiency and adherence to prescribed policies are promoted.
observations.managementAction: Appointed Internal Auditor

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management that:

1.) Require the management to provide effective fence to control entrance of unwanted animals.
2.) Require management to properly process the wastes in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003.
3.) Require the management to maintain a basic record of the Sanitary Landfill

We recommended to Management that:
1.) Strengthen controls in the use of motor vehicles by complying with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 and Section 361.e of the GAAM.
2.) Require management to regulate the use of government vehicle, monitor and control trip tickets in chronological manner.
3.) Proper markings of the vehicles with the name and/or logo of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” should be made pursuant to Joint Circular No. 1 dated September 3, 1991 of the COA and DBM to prevent unauthorized use of the same.
4.) Require the driver to accomplish the Driver’s Trip Ticket. They should always provide data on estimated distance travelled, balance in tank, fuel issued, fuel used and balance in tank at the end of travel.
5.) Require drivers to prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels per vehicle summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month.
6.) Require the preparation and submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor within the first 10 days for verification purposes.  The report shall show among others, the different types of motor vehicles utilized during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle; and
7.) The driver of motor vehicle concerned should be required to report defective odometer/speedometer so that repair or replacement could be undertaken promptly.
observations.managementAction: Complied trip tickets and logo markings

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management that:

1.) Direct the BAC Secretariat to comply strictly with the submission of contract/s and POs by observing the prescribed period within which to submit the procurement documents. 
2.) Integrate to the BAC Secretariat Office some of the existing procurement personnel so that it can supplement the existing manpower complement on said office, and efficient conduct on the procurement activities including (1) canvass of prices, (2) monitoring of the contracts/POs already served or received by the contractor/supplier and (3) its ultimate submission to COA, (4) assist in the preparation of procurement documents and (5) review of the procurement documents before they are approved by the Head Of the Procuring Entity or its authorized representative, could be attained.
3.) Henceforth, submit the 2016 contracts/POs executed within 5 working days after perfection of the contract in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended to Management that:

Strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. 9184 to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan and the requirements under DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6A to ensure inclusion of agency procurement activities for common-use supplies and equipment in the consolidated APP-CSE.




We recommended to Management that:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the provisions of R.A. 9184 particularly the posting of procurement results in the Government Electronic Procurement System (G-EPS) bulletin board, GPPB Circular No. 01-2005 and DILG Memo Circular No. 2010-83.

We recommended to Management that:

The Municipal Treasurer and the  Municipal General Services Officer to perform a course of action relative to the disposal of unserviceable properties as soon as possible in accordance with Section 163 of COA Circular No. 92-386 in order to prevent further deterioration and make available the space occupied by the said properties.

We have recommended the following. 

1. Observe strictly the guidelines set forth under Section 89  of P.D. 1445 	and COA Circular No. 97-002.
2. Monitor closely cash advances and require the liquidation of a previous 	cash advance, or the proper accounting thereof, before another cash advance is granted.
3. Require the concerned officials/employees to liquidate their cash 	advances as soon as the purpose for which these were given, had been served.
4. Stop the grant of additional cash advances to officials/employees unless 	previous cash advances are first settled.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Finding number 13.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following. 

1. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Inventory Team to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the municipality at least once a year, prepare the report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and to submit it to the Auditor as prescribed in the manual;
The  Property Officer should maintain a record of property card for each item of PPE; and
Reconcile the results of the actual physical count against the balances reflected in its financial statements so that determined variances shall be properly recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Property Officer-Designate required for the submission of list of all property, plant and equipment by office but failed to conduct physical inventory.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Finding number 14.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following. 

1. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the unserviceable properties from the Property, Plant and Equipment to Other Assets account.
2. The Municipal Treasurer or the  Municipal General Services Officer to perform a course of action relative to the disposal of unserviceable properties as soon as possible in accordance with Section 163 of COA Circular No. 92-386 in order to prevent further deterioration and make available the space occupied by the said properties.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order will be issued to the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the unserviceable properties from the Property, Plant and Equipment to Other Assets account; and
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Finding number 15.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended that management instructs the property officer to maintain a     record of property card for each   item of PPE; conduct physical count of all PPEs owned       by the agency, through its duly designated members of the Inventory Committee,   at least once a year and reconcile accounts between the  Accounting Unit and the    Property Officer from time to time.

We recommended  that management  cconduct  monthly monitoring of liquidation of cash advances; refrain from allowing municipal personnel for additional cash advance unless their previous cash advance is settled and impose administrative disciplinary action, to municipal officials and employee for the settlement of their outstanding accounts/liquidation of their respective cash advances after proper written demands were made.

We recommended that management  secure adequate fidelity bond equivalent to  the maximum accountability of accountable officer in order to safeguard the assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation and other loss due to fortuitous events; monitor properly the change in accountability of each   accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premium conforms to their amount of bond and appropriate yearly a budget to cover for the payment of premiums on the renewal of the fidelity bonds both for public funds and public property.

We recommended that management should designate disbursing responsibility only to those who are duly bonded to afford adequate protection to the government and strictly comply with Section 182 of the GAAM; Volume I, the regulations governing the Fidelity Bonding of government officials and employees under Treasury Order No. 01-95 of the Bureau of Treasury should also be implemented.

We recommended to management to require the barangay officials to assist municipal Treasury’s Office personnel in delivering Real Property Tax Statements (RPTS) to the 16 barangays of the municipality and to assist in locating the owner of the undelivered RPTS; direct the Municipality Treasurer to avail of the civil remedies provided for under R.A. 7160 and develop other feasible strategies to enhance collections of delinquent taxes from taxpayers.

We recommended to management to facilitate the adoption of Payroll-thru-bank System, which is a much safer scheme that precludes hold-ups and similar incidents involving payrolls, and promotes operational efficiency.

We recommended that management require the AO to prepare monthly the Report of Accountability for AFs and submit the same to the municipal treasurer; require the municipal treasurer to prepare the consolidated Inventory and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit it to the Unit Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that management direct the accounting office to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the general ledger controlling accounts for easy verification and control of the account and facilitate review of transactions and events recorded; and improve the present accounting system by installing computerized system of the accounting office that will provide adequate data control and present accurate reports.

We recommended that management install an Internal Audit Service to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of R.A. No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278. Moreover, it should be required to regularly evaluate and/or monitor the implementation of its accounting and operational controls in all aspects of its operations to ensure that reliability of data, assets and records are safeguarded, and that operational efficiency and adherence to prescribed policies are promoted.

We recommended that management require the management to provide        effective fence to control entrance of unwanted animals; properly process the wastes and maintain a basic record of the Sanitary Landfill in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003.

We recommended that sstrengthen controls in the use of motor vehicles by      complying with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 and Section 361.e of the GAAM such as issuance of pre-numbered trip tickets; ensure proper markings of the vehicles with the name and/or logo of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” should be made pursuant to Joint Circular No. 1 dated September 3, 1991 of the COA and DBM to prevent unauthorized use of the same. Moreover,  require the driver to accomplish the Driver’s Trip Ticket, submit Monthly Report of Official Travels per vehicle summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month and also the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor within the first 10 days for verification purposes.

We recommended to  management to  direct the BAC Secretariat to comply       strictly with the submission of contract/s and POs by observing the prescribed period within which to submit the procurement documents; integrate to the BAC Secretariat Office some of the existing procurement personnel so that it can supplement the existing manpower complement on said office, and efficient conduct on the procurement activities including (1) canvass of prices, (2) monitoring of the contracts/POs already served or received by the contractor/supplier and (3) its ultimate submission to COA, (4) assist in the preparation of procurement documents and (5) review of the procurement documents before they are approved by the Head Of the Procuring Entity or its authorized representative, could be attained and  submit the 2016 contracts/POs executed within 5 working days after perfection of the contract in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended to management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the provisions of R.A. 9184 particularly the posting of procurement results in the Government Electronic Procurement System (G-EPS) bulletin board, GPPB Circular No. 01-2005 and DILG Memo Circular No. 2010-83.

We recommended to management to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal General Services Officer to dispose thef unserviceable properties as soon as possible in accordance with Section 163 of COA Circular No. 92-386 in order to prevent further deterioration and make available the space occupied by the said properties.

We recommended that management prepare and submit its annual GAD plan and budget to the NCRFW for review and endorsement to the DBM and refer  to the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), National Economic and Development Auditory (NEDA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular No. 12091 which superseded the DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 which took effect in CY 2013.

We have recommended the following. 

Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the monetization of leaves.
Consider the formulation of policy on the order of availment of leave credit monetization of 50% or more based on justifiable reasons.
The Agency should stop the granting of monetization out of sick leave credits
The Agency should observe the five-day retention limit in the monetization of vacation leave credits.
Strictly conform to the provision that monetization of leave credits per employee will be availed of only once a year.
Instruct the Leave in Charge to make necessary adjustment in the leave cards of an employee whose monetizationwas deducted from the sick leave instead of vacation leave.
observations.managementAction:
Rules and regulations on the monetization of leaves should be strictly observed.
Local policy shall be formulated on availment of leave credit monetization.
Monetization sourced out from sick leave credits shall no longer be allowed.
Five-day retention limit should be strictly observed in monetization of vacation leave credits.
Provision that monetization of leave credits per employee will be availed of only once a year shall be strictly conformed.
The Human Resource Management Officer-Designate shall make necessary adjustment in the leave cards of an employee whose monetization was deducted from the sick leave instead of vacation leave.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following. 

We have recommended that the municipality should enter into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the respective beneficiary/recipient stipulating therein the purpose of fund transfer and the obligations of both parties which include, among others, the submission of liquidation reports by the recipient to the municipality.

The municipality should strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2007-001 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers to NGOs/POs/LGUs/CSOs.
